sourceMovie.title: A Chinese Ghost Story
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: A Chinese Ghost Story works as a fantasy movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The fantasy and action blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Kung Fu Hustle
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If A Chinese Ghost Story clicked for you, Kung Fu Hustle (2004) is a strong next stop: both trade in fantasy and action, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after A Chinese Ghost Story?
faq.answer: Try Kung Fu Hustle, Damsel, The Forbidden Kingdom—each shares DNA with A Chinese Ghost Story while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is A Chinese Ghost Story?
faq.answer: A Chinese Ghost Story (1987) is best described as Fantasy, Action. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is A Chinese Ghost Story part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "A Chinese Ghost Story Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
